last_action: all 9 tasks done — 316 extension tests + 1596 jest tests pass, shape parity OK

FR-001.title: Do not descend into a directory that contains its own project configuration

FR-002.title: Apply the boundary identically to the orphans and full-inventory listings

FR-003.title: The scan root's own configuration is not a boundary against itself

FR-004.title: A nested project with living specs switched off contributes nothing

FR-005.title: An unreadable nested configuration still establishes a boundary

FR-006.title: No file is reported as both a claimed capability and unclaimed

FR-007.title: Discovered inventory entries carry distinct, non-colliding names

FR-008.title: Behavior is unchanged when there are no nested projects

FR-009.title: Regression tests cover boundary, opt-out, listing agreement, and names

FR-010.title: Record the change in the extension changelog and documentation

context: constraint: drift.py is already correct — match its configured-only behavior
constraint: feature branch must not bump extension.yml version
intent: Stop living-spec discovery at nested project boundaries so a repo with sample projects reports only its own specs
expectations: No change to drift.py, which already iterates only configured capabilities
No new marker file — the existing project configuration marks the boundary
No version bump to extension.yml and no root README/CHANGELOG/package.json changes

T001.did: Added find_spec_files: an os.walk that prunes any subdirectory carrying its own .specify/companion.yml

T002.did: find_orphans now iterates the boundary-aware walk instead of a recursive glob

T003.did: discover_all reuses find_orphans' file set, so the inventory and the orphan list share one scan

T004.did: Discovered names widen along the path until distinct and never displace a configured capability

T005.did: Added ProjectBoundaryTests: boundary stop, opt-out, unreadable nested config, depth, root-not-a-boundary, boundary inside an owned capability dir

T006.did: Added DiscoveryConsistencyTests: no overlap between claimed and orphan paths, unique discovered names, configured names untouched

T007.did: Documented the nested-project boundary in the living-specs discovery section

T008.did: Added the [Unreleased] Fixed entries for the boundary, listing agreement, and name collisions

T009.did: Ran the extension python suites, npm compile/test, shape parity, and re-ran the original reproduction

verified.result: 147 living-spec tests pass; 316 across all six suites

verified.what: VS Code extension build and unit tests

verified.result: OK — 14 bodies match parts, golden, and carry the timing fence
nested project and opted-out sandbox files no longer reported; a genuine root-level orphan still is

verified.what: new tests fail without the fix
verified.result: 6/10 boundary tests and 2/3 naming tests fail as expected

decisions.decision: The boundary is the presence of the config file, not what it says
decisions.why: A project that declares itself is a project; this also makes an unreadable or empty nested config stop the scan instead of letting it descend
decisions.rejected: Reading each nested config and only stopping when it enables or disables living specs

decisions.decision: Both listings read one shared file walk
decisions.why: The reported self-contradiction was two code paths answering the same question; one scan makes disagreement unrepresentable rather than merely fixed
decisions.rejected: Adding the boundary check separately to each listing

decisions.decision: Colliding discovered names widen along the path until distinct
decisions.why: Keeps every discovered spec addressable instead of dropping one, and never displaces a configured capability name
decisions.rejected: Numeric suffixes, or skipping the second colliding entry

decisions.decision: The walk keeps skipping dot-directories and dotfiles
decisions.why: Preserves byte-for-byte behavior on repos with no nested projects, matching the glob it replaced
decisions.rejected: Walking everything, which would newly surface specs under hidden directories

implement.summary: Living-spec discovery now stops at nested projects, both listings share one scan, and discovered names are unique
